content.text: Answer 4 questions. You should indicate which 4 problems you wish to be graded. Write your solutions clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote theorems (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

problem.text: In \(S_8\), write down a representative of each conjugacy class of elements of order \(4\) and compute the order of its centralizer.
Let \(G\) be a group of order \(124=2^2\cdot 31\). Prove that \(G\) has a cyclic subgroup of order \(62\).
Prove that there exists a nonabelian group of order \(2025=3^4\cdot 5^2\). (You do not have to determine its multiplication explicitly.)

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Give the definition of a maximal subgroup of a nontrivial group.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Find two maximal subgroups of \(S_5\) of different orders.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Show that the group \((\mathbb{Q},+)\) has no maximal subgroup.

problem.text: Let \(G\) be a finite group and \(p\) a prime. Let \(K\) be the intersection of all the Sylow \(p\)-subgroups of \(G\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that \(K\) is a characteristic subgroup of \(G\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Suppose \(H\) is a normal \(p\)-subgroup of \(G\). Prove that \(H\subseteq K\).

problem.text: Let \(G\) be a group, and let \(Z(G)\) denote its center.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: State what it means for \(G\) to be nilpotent of class \(c\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(G\) is nilpotent of class \(1\), then \(G\) is abelian.

subparts.label: (d)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(G\) is nilpotent of class \(2\), then \(G'\subseteq Z(G)\).
